title: Even Larger Amount Of Global Grain Exports Expected
section_path: Even Larger Amount Of Global Grain Exports Expected
section_path_text: Even Larger Amount Of Global Grain Exports Expected
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
Global seaborne grain trade prospects for the current 2021/22 season have long remained very bullish, and an even larger amount of exports are now expected. The United States Department of Agriculture has released their latest forecasts for 2021/22 and is now forecasting global grain exports will total 504.2 million tons. This is 5.2 million tons (1%) more than was forecast a month ago and would mark a year-on-year increase of 25.7 million tons (5%).
